sections.label: Section 671
sections.original: ταύτης ἱκνοῦμαί σʼ — ὦ μέλεος ἐμῶν κακῶν ,
sections.translation: As Menelaus turns away . Ah me , my misery ! to what have I come ! Well ? ( preparing to make a final appeal ) I must suffer , for I am making this appeal on behalf of my whole family . O my uncle , my father ’ s own brother ! Imagine that the dead man in his grave

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: θανόντʼ ἀκούειν τάδε δόκει , ποτωμένην
sections.translation: is listening , that his spirit is hovering over you and saying what I say , this much for tears and groans and misfortunes . I have spoken and I have begged for my safety , hunting what all seek , not myself alone .

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: κἀγώ σʼ ἱκνοῦμαι καὶ γυνή περ οὖσʼ ὅμως
sections.translation: I , too , though I am only a woman , beseech you to help those who need it ; for you have the power .

sections.label: Section 682
sections.original: Ὀρέστʼ , ἐγώ τοι σὸν καταιδοῦμαι κάρα
sections.translation: Orestes , you are a man for whom I have a deep regard , and I want to take part in your troubles ; it is a duty , too , to help relatives bear their ills ,

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: ξυνεκκομίζειν , δύναμιν ἢν διδῷ θεός ,
sections.translation: by dying or killing enemies , if god gives the power to do so . I wish I had that power granted me by the gods . For I have come quite destitute of allies , after my long weary wanderings ,

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: σμικρᾷ σὺν ἀλκῇ τῶν λελειμμένων φίλων .
sections.translation: with the small strength of my surviving friends . We should never get the better of Pelasgian Argos by fighting ; if we should prevail by soothing speeches , we will come to some hope there . For how can you win a great cause by small

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: πόνοισιν ; ἀμαθὲς καὶ τὸ βούλεσθαι τάδε .
sections.translation: efforts ? It is foolish even to wish it .

sections.label: Section 696
sections.original: ὅταν γὰρ ἡβᾷ δῆμος εἰς ὀργὴν πεσών ,
sections.translation: For when the people fall into a vigorous fury , they are as hard to quench as a raging fire ; but if you gently slacken your hold and yield a little to their tension , cautiously watching your opportunity ,

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: ἴσως ἂν ἐκπνεύσειεν · ἢν δʼ ἀνῇ πνοάς ,
sections.translation: they may possibly calm down ; if their gusts abate , you may obtain whatever you want from them easily . They have pity , and a hot temper too , an invaluable quality if you watch it closely . So for you I will go and try to persuade Tyndareus

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: πόλιν τε πεῖσαι τῷ λίαν χρῆσθαι καλῶς .
sections.translation: and the city to moderation . A ship also dips if its sheet is hauled too taut , but rights itself again if it is let go .

sections.label: Section 708
sections.original: μισεῖ γὰρ ὁ θεὸς τὰς ἄγαν προθυμίας ,
sections.translation: The god hates excessive eagerness , and the citizens do also ; I must save you , I don ’ t deny it ,

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original: σῴζειν σε σοφίᾳ , μὴ βίᾳ τῶν κρεισσόνων .
sections.translation: by cleverness , not by violence against those who are stronger . I could not do it by strength , as you perhaps imagine ; for it is not easy to triumph single-handed over the troubles that beset you . I would never have tried to bring the Argive land over to softness ;

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: προσηγόμεσθα · νῦν δʼ ἀναγκαίως ἔχει .
sections.translation: but it is necessary . for the wise to be slaves to fortune . Exit Menelaus .

sections.label: Section 717
sections.original: ὦ πλὴν γυναικὸς οὕνεκα στρατηλατεῖν
sections.translation: O you that have no use , except to lead an army in a woman ’ s cause ! O worst of men in your friends ’ defense ,

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: φεύγεις ἀποστραφείς με , τὰ δʼ Ἀγαμέμνονος
sections.translation: do you turn your back on me and flee , the deeds of Agamemnon lost and gone ?

sections.label: Section 721
sections.original: φροῦδʼ ; ἄφιλος ἦσθʼ ἄρʼ , ὦ πάτερ , πράσσων κακῶς .
sections.translation: After all , father , you had no friends in adversity . Alas ! I am betrayed ; no longer do I have any hope of finding a refuge where I may escape the death-sentence of Argos ; for this man was my haven of safety .

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰσορῶ γὰρ τόνδε φίλτατον βροτῶν
sections.translation: But I see Pylades , the best of friends , coming at a run from Phocis — a pleasant sight ! A man who can be trusted in troubles is a better sight than a calm to sailors .

sections.label: Section 729
sections.original: θᾶσσον ᾗ με χρῆν προβαίνων ἱκόμην διʼ ἄστεως ,
sections.translation: I have come through the city quickly , as I should ,

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: σύλλογον πόλεως ἀκούσας , τὸν δʼ ἰδὼν αὐτὸς σαφῶς ,
sections.translation: having heard and myself clearly seen the citizens assembling , against you and your sister , to kill you at once . What is happening ? How is it with you ? How are you doing , my best of comrades , friends and kin ? For you are all these to me .

sections.label: Section 734
sections.original: οἰχόμεσθʼ , ὡς ἐν βραχεῖ σοι τἀμὰ δηλώσω κακά .
sections.translation: I am ruined , to make plain to you my troubles in brief .

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: συγκατασκάπτοις ἂν ἡμᾶς · κοινὰ γὰρ τὰ τῶν φίλων .
sections.translation: You must destroy me also ; for friends have all in common .

sections.label: Section 736
sections.original: Μενέλεως κάκιστος ἐς ἐμὲ καὶ κασιγνήτην ἐμήν .
sections.translation: Menelaus is the worst of men to me and my sister .

sections.label: Section 737
sections.original: εἰκότως , κακῆς γυναικὸς ἄνδρα γίγνεσθαι κακόν .
sections.translation: It is natural for the husband of an evil woman to become evil .

sections.label: Section 738
sections.original: ὥσπερ οὐκ ἐλθὼν ἔμοιγε ταὐτὸν ἀπέδωκεν μολών .
sections.translation: He no more repaid me by coming here , than if he had never come .

sections.label: Section 739
sections.original: ἦ γάρ ἐστιν ὡς ἀληθῶς τήνδʼ ἀφιγμένος χθόνα ;
sections.translation: Oh , has he really arrived in this land ?

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: χρόνιος · ἀλλʼ ὅμως τάχιστα κακὸς ἐφωράθη φίλοις .
sections.translation: He took a long time , but he was very soon detected as evil to his friends .

sections.label: Section 741
sections.original: καὶ δάμαρτα τὴν κακίστην ναυστολῶν ἐλήλυθεν ;
sections.translation: And did he bring his wife , the worst of women , with him on his ship ?

sections.label: Section 742
sections.original: οὐκ ἐκεῖνος , ἀλλʼ ἐκείνη κεῖνον ἐνθάδʼ ἤγαγεν .
sections.translation: It was not he who brought her here , but she who brought him .

sections.label: Section 743
sections.original: ποῦ ʼστιν ἣ πλείστους Ἀχαιῶν ὤλεσεν γυνὴ μία ;
sections.translation: Where is she , the one woman who proved the ruin of so many Achaeans ?

sections.label: Section 744
sections.original: ἐν δόμοις ἐμοῖσιν , εἰ δὴ τούσδʼ ἐμοὺς καλεῖν χρεών .
sections.translation: In my house ; if , that is , I ought to call it mine .

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: σὺ δὲ τίνας λόγους ἔλεξας σοῦ κασιγνήτῳ πατρός ;
sections.translation: And what did you say to your father ’ s brother ?

sections.label: Section 746
sections.original: μή μʼ ἰδεῖν θανόνθʼ ὑπʼ ἀστῶν καὶ κασιγνήτην ἐμήν .
sections.translation: Not to see me and my sister killed by the citizens .

sections.label: Section 747
sections.original: πρὸς θεῶν , τί πρὸς τάδʼ εἶπε ; τόδε γὰρ εἰδέναι θέλω .
sections.translation: By the gods ! What did he say to that ? I would like know this .

sections.label: Section 748
sections.original: εὐλαβεῖθʼ , ὃ τοῖς φίλοισι δρῶσιν οἱ κακοὶ φίλοι .
sections.translation: He was cautious , the usual policy of ignoble friends .

sections.label: Section 749
sections.original: σκῆψιν ἐς ποίαν προβαίνων ; τοῦτο πάντʼ ἔχω μαθών .
sections.translation: What excuse did he advance ? When I have learned that , I know everything .

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: οὗτος ἦλθʼ , ὁ τὰς ἀρίστας θυγατέρας σπείρας πατήρ .
sections.translation: There was a new arrival , the father who begot those noble daughters .

sections.label: Section 751
sections.original: Τυνδάρεων λέγεις · ἴσως σοι θυγατέρος θυμούμενος ;
sections.translation: You mean Tyndareus ; he was angry with you , perhaps , for his daughter ’ s sake ?

sections.label: Section 752
sections.original: αἰσθάνῃ . τὸ τοῦδε κῆδος μᾶλλον εἵλετʼ ἢ πατρός .
sections.translation: You understand . And Menelaus preferred the family relationship with him to that with my father .

sections.label: Section 753
sections.original: κοὐκ ἐτόλμησεν πόνων σῶν ἀντιλάζυσθαι παρών ;
sections.translation: He did not have the courage to share your troubles , when he was here ?

sections.label: Section 754
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ αἰχμητὴς πέφυκεν , ἐν γυναιξὶ δʼ ἄλκιμος .
sections.translation: No , for he was not born a warrior , though strong among women !

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: ἐν κακοῖς ἄρʼ εἶ μεγίστοις · καί σʼ ἀναγκαῖον θανεῖν ;
sections.translation: Your case is desperate , it seems , and you must die .

sections.label: Section 756
sections.original: ψῆφον ἀμφʼ ἡμῶν πολίτας ἐπὶ φόνῳ θέσθαι χρεών .
sections.translation: The citizens must give their vote about us on the murder .

sections.label: Section 757
sections.original: ἣ κρινεῖ τί χρῆμα ; λέξον · διὰ φόβου γὰρ ἔρχομαι .
sections.translation: And what is that to decide ? Tell me , for I am alarmed .

sections.label: Section 758
sections.original: ἢ θανεῖν ἢ ζῆν · ὁ μῦθος οὐ μακρὸς μακρῶν πέρι .
sections.translation: Our life or death ; a brief speech on a large subject .

sections.label: Section 759
sections.original: φεῦγέ νυν λιπὼν μέλαθρα σὺν κασιγνήτῃ σέθεν .
sections.translation: Leave the palace with your sister now and try to escape .

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: οὐχ ὁρᾷς ; φυλασσόμεσθα φρουρίοισι πανταχῇ .
sections.translation: Don ’ t you see ? We are being watched by guards on every side .

sections.label: Section 761
sections.original: εἶδον ἄστεως ἀγυιὰς τεύχεσιν πεφραγμένας .
sections.translation: I saw that the streets of the city were secured with armed men .

sections.label: Section 762
sections.original: ὡσπερεὶ πόλις πρὸς ἐχθρῶν σῶμα πυργηρούμεθα .
sections.translation: We are as closely beleaguered as a city by its foes .

sections.label: Section 763
sections.original: κἀμὲ νῦν ἐροῦ τί πάσχω · καὶ γὰρ αὐτὸς οἴχομαι .
sections.translation: Ask me now of my state ; for I too am ruined .

sections.label: Section 764
sections.original: πρὸς τίνος ; τοῦτʼ ἂν προσείη τοῖς ἐμοῖς κακοῖς κακόν .
sections.translation: By whom ? This would be a further trouble to add to mine .

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: Στρόφιος ἤλασέν μʼ ἀπʼ οἴκων φυγάδα θυμωθεὶς πατήρ .
sections.translation: Strophius , my father , in a fit of anger , has banished me from his house .

sections.label: Section 766
sections.original: ἴδιον ἢ κοινὸν πολίταις ἐπιφέρων ἔγκλημά τι ;
sections.translation: Bringing against you a private charge , or one in which the citizens share ?

sections.label: Section 767
sections.original: ὅτι συνηράμην φόνον σοι μητρός , ἀνόσιον λέγων .
sections.translation: He says it is an unholy crime to have helped you slay your mother .

sections.label: Section 768
sections.original: ὦ τάλας , ἔοικε καὶ σὲ τἀμὰ λυπήσειν κακά .
sections.translation: Alas ! It seems my troubles will cause you grief as well .

sections.label: Section 769
sections.original: οὐχὶ Μενέλεω τρόποισι χρώμεθʼ · οἰστέον τάδε .
sections.translation: I am not like Menelaus in character ; this must be endured .

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: οὐ φοβῇ μή σʼ Ἄργος ὥσπερ κἄμʼ ἀποκτεῖναι θέλῃ ;
sections.translation: Are you not afraid that Argos will desire your death as well as mine ?

sections.label: Section 771
sections.original: οὐ προσήκομεν κολάζειν τοῖσδε , Φωκέων δὲ γῇ .
sections.translation: I am not theirs to punish ; I belong to Phocis .

sections.label: Section 772
sections.original: δεινὸν οἱ πολλοί , κακούργους ὅταν ἔχωσι προστάτας .
sections.translation: A terrible thing is the mob , whenever it has villains to lead it .

sections.label: Section 773
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὅταν χρηστοὺς λάβωσι , χρηστὰ βουλεύουσʼ ἀεί .
sections.translation: But with honest leaders its counsels are always honest .

sections.label: Section 774
sections.original: εἶἑν . ἐς κοινὸν λέγειν χρή .
sections.translation: Very well ; we must consult together .
